assertions.description: Total 46 components correctly identified: 9 resistors, 6 capacitors, 6 jumpers, 4 test points, 4 fiducials, 4 NMOS transistors (Q1-Q4 RE1C002UNTCL), 2 ICs (U1 HM01B0, U2 RT9080), 2 mounting holes, 1 LED, 1 active oscillator (Y1), 3 other. All counts match the schematic.

U2 (RT9080-2.8V) correctly identified as LDO regulator with input_rail=3.3V and output_rail=2.8V. vref_source=fixed_suffix is appropriate since output voltage is encoded in part name. Decoupling capacitors on 2.8V (C8, C2, C7, C5) correctly attributed.

Y1 (24MHz, SparkFun-Clock:Oscillator_24MHz_3.2x2.5mm) correctly classified as active_oscillator. It is a 4-pin package with pins GND/VDD/OUT/EN — an active MEMS/clock oscillator, not a passive crystal. No load_caps reported which is correct: active oscillators have internal load caps.

Q1-Q4 (RE1C002UNTCL NMOS) all 4 correctly detected. Q1 (gate=2.8V, drain=SDA, source=CAM_SDA) and Q2 (gate=2.8V, drain=SCL, source=CAM_SCL) are bidirectional I2C level shifters. Q3/Q4 are camera power/control switches (PWDN, RST). All captured in transistor_circuits.

Power rails correctly identified: 3.3V (host-supplied input), 2.8V (from RT9080 LDO), 1.5V (camera supply rail), GND. All four domains present. Decoupling analysis covers all three voltage rails accurately.

I2C bus not detected despite two I2C buses present: SCL/SDA (3.3V host domain, J1/J2/J3 connectors) and CAM_SCL/CAM_SDA (2.8V camera domain, J4 connector) bridged by Q1/Q2 level-shift MOSFETs. R1/R2 (2.2k) are pull-ups for SDA/SCL connected through JP1 (SolderJumper_3_Bridged123, center pin B = 3.3V). bus_analysis.i2c is empty.

I2C pull-ups R1/R2 (2.2k) not recognized because they connect to 3.3V through JP1 solder jumper (SolderJumper_3_Bridged123). The pull-up path is: R1/R2 pin 2 -> unnamed net -> JP1 pin C -> JP1 pin B -> 3.3V. The analyzer does not trace through solder jumpers to find pull-up supply rails.
